name: Villages of La Serena: Peñalsordo and Capilla — circular from Cabeza Del Buey
name_original: Pueblos de la Serena: Peñalsordo y Capilla — circular desde Cabeza Del Buey
region_intro: Road cycling around Cabeza Del Buey offers varied terrain, situated on the northern slopes of the Sierra del Pedregoso, an extension of the Sierra Morena. The region features a mix of steppes, quartzitic highlands, and dehesas, providing diverse backdrops for rides. Significant elevations, such as Tiros (957 meters) and Sierra de la Rinconada (844 meters), contribute to engaging routes with climbs and descents. This topography ensures a range of road cycling experiences across well-paved sections. Best road cycling routes around Cabeza Del Buey The most popular road cycling route is Monterrubio de la Serena from Bellacázar — circular via Cabeza del Buey, a 55.0 miles (88.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This circular route passes directly through Cabeza del Buey. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santa Eufemia Loop from Belalcázar, a moderate 45.7 miles (73.6 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the Los Pedroches region with varied topography. Local road cyclists also love the Villages of La Serena: Peñalsordo and Capilla — circular from Cabeza Del Buey, a 60.1 miles (96.7 km) trail leading through the Villages of La Serena, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Road cycling around Cabeza Del Buey is defined by the Sierra del Pedregoso foothills, extensive dehesas, and quartzitic highlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cabeza Del Buey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cabeza Del Buey?
A: There are 5 road cycling routes available around Cabeza Del Buey on komoot, catering to various skill levels. These include 1 easy route, 1 moderate route, and 3 difficult routes, offering diverse experiences across the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The terrain around Cabeza Del Buey is quite varied, situated on the northern slopes of the Sierra del Pedregoso, an extension of the Sierra Morena. You'll encounter a mix of steppes, quartzitic highlands, and dehesas. Significant elevations, such as Tiros (957 meters) and Sierra de la Rinconada (844 meters), contribute to engaging routes with rewarding climbs and descents on well-paved sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. While the guide highlights more challenging options, the varied topography ensures that riders can find paths that match their comfort and experience levels.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993792972/villages-of-la-serena-penalsordo-and-capilla-circular-from-cabeza-del-buey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villages of La Serena: Peñalsordo and Capilla — circular from Cabeza Del Buey&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 96.7 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016959451/almaden-from-belalcazar-circular-via-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaden from Belalcazar — circular via Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 126.2 km with significant climbs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cabeza Del Buey are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016973219/monterrubio-de-la-serena-from-bellacazar-circular-via-cabeza-del-buey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monterrubio de la Serena from Bellacázar — circular via Cabeza del Buey&lt;/a&gt; is an 88.5 km loop that passes directly through Cabeza del Buey.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Given its location in southern Spain, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Cabeza Del Buey. Temperatures are milder, and the landscapes are often at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect on road cycling routes in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: The region offers several scenic viewpoints. You can enjoy panoramic views of the La Serena Reservoir, especially from areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cerro Masatrigo from Mirador Celeste de Galizuela&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt; also provides stunning vistas of the surrounding natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can encounter historical architecture such as the 16th-century Church of Nuestra Señora de Armentera and the significant Sanctuary of Belén. Ancient structures like the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belalcázar Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; offer spectacular views and a glimpse into the past. The old town's cobbled streets and charming squares in Cabeza Del Buey itself are also worth exploring.

Q: What natural features will I encounter while cycling?
A: Road cycling routes in Cabeza Del Buey will take you through diverse natural environments. You'll experience the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra del Pedregoso foothills, extensive dehesas (wooded pastures), and quartzitic highlands. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent natural feature, offering picturesque views along some routes.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling options available?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for longer rides, the region offers routes exceeding 100 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016959451/almaden-from-belalcazar-circular-via-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaden from Belalcazar — circular via Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is 126.2 km long, providing a substantial challenge for endurance cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Cabeza Del Buey?
A: Road cyclists using komoot often praise the varied terrain and the engaging nature of the routes. The combination of challenging climbs and scenic descents through diverse landscapes, from steppes to dehesas, is frequently highlighted. The region's unique natural beauty and historical points of interest also contribute to a rewarding cycling experience.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Los Pedroches region?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of the Los Pedroches region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016791655/santa-eufemia-loop-from-belalcazar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eufemia Loop from Belalcázar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 73.6 km path specifically noted for its scenic views of Los Pedroches, featuring varied topography and long, straight sections.

Q: Are there routes that pass through multiple villages?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to connect different villages, allowing you to experience the local culture and architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993792972/villages-of-la-serena-penalsordo-and-capilla-circular-from-cabeza-del-buey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villages of La Serena: Peñalsordo and Capilla — circular from Cabeza Del Buey&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you through multiple charming settlements in the La Serena region.
tips: Capilla is a locality that is very close to the Serena Reservoir and within what is called the Sierra de Palanque, a small set of hills that go from the neighboring town of Peñalsordo to Capilla. It is a small town with few inhabitants.
Beautiful town in the east of the province of Badajoz where it is surrounded by small ranges or hills that give a change of trend to the typical Extremadura plains.
Small mountain pass that is close to the town of Cabeza de Buey and that separates the two mountainous areas in the area. It separates the Sierra de la Osa from the Sierra de las Vacas.
